mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of tightening periods with falling INDPRO, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising employment signals an overheating economy that forces aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth consistently correlates with higher corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward quarterly returns are negative in >55% of positive GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 in a single month indicates severe economic weakness, triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >50% of payroll decline events, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment demonstrates strong economic momentum, driving immediate positive equity returns as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over independent predictors.
